pages.name: Home
pages.description: Entry point for the children’s therapy organization, highlighting key services, tools, and quick actions for families. Provides direct CTAs into core tasks like scheduling evaluations, finding therapists, exploring resources, registering for groups, estimating costs, and donating.
primary_functions: Introduce the organization’s mission and core therapy services for children.
Provide clear entry points to Services, Our Team, Resources, Groups, Locations, Pricing, Planner, and Donate.
Offer prominent call-to-action buttons such as “Schedule a Speech Evaluation,” “Find a Therapist,” and “Find a Clinic Near Me.”
Surface quick links or featured content like social skills groups, sensory processing articles, and donation campaigns.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on “Services” in header navigation or service-related CTA in hero/featured sections.
Click on a featured service card (e.g., Speech Therapy).
Click on hero CTA “Schedule a Speech Evaluation”.
Click on hero CTA “Find a Therapist” or “Our Team” header link.
Click on a featured resource/article card.
Click on a featured group/class card.
Click on hero CTA “Find a Clinic Near Me”.
Click on “Pricing & Insurance” in header or pricing teaser content.
Click on “My Planner” in header or planner promo block.
Click on a featured donation campaign CTA.
Click on organization intro link such as “Learn more about us”.

access_methods.description: Reached by clicking the “Home” logo/label in the main header navigation.
Default landing page when visiting the root domain.

pages.name: Services Overview
pages.description: Overview of all therapy services offered, grouped and described for parents deciding what kind of help their child might need.
primary_functions: List all therapy services with brief descriptions and age ranges served.
Allow users to click on a specific service (e.g., “Speech Therapy”) to open its detail page (service_detail.html?serviceId=speech_therapy).
Provide per-service quick actions such as “Schedule an Evaluation” linking to the appointment booking page with the service preselected.
Cross-link to related content such as groups, resources, and pricing information for each service.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a service name or “Learn more” link.
Click on “Schedule an Evaluation” on a service card.
Click on “View groups & classes for this service” link on a service card.
Click on “Parent resources for this service”.
Click on “View pricing for this service”.

access_methods.description: Click the “Services” item in the main header navigation.
Click the “Therapy Services” link in the footer.
Click a services teaser or CTA from the Home page or About page.

pages.name: Service Detail
pages.description: Detailed information about a specific therapy service such as Speech Therapy or Occupational Therapy, including goals, methods, what to expect, and related offerings.
primary_functions: Display in-depth information about the selected service (goals, methods, age groups, what to expect).
Show practical details like typical session length, frequency, and whether group options exist.
Provide a prominent “Schedule an Evaluation” or “Request Appointment” button that leads to appointment_booking.html with the service type preselected.
Highlight related therapists, groups, and parent resources relevant to this service.

incoming_params.description: Identifier of the service to display (e.g., the Speech Therapy service).

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on the primary “Schedule an Evaluation” button when evaluations are available.
Click on a secondary “Request Ongoing Appointments” button if shown.
Click on “View therapists for this service” within related therapists section.
Click on a related group/class teaser or “View all groups for this service”.
Click on “See more parent resources about this topic”.
Click on “See pricing & insurance for this service”.

access_methods.description: Accessed when a link includes ?serviceId=... from Services Overview, Home, or related content.

pages.name: Appointment Booking
pages.description: Unified page for booking initial evaluations and requesting appointments with services and/or specific therapists, including slot-based evaluation booking.
primary_functions: Allow users to select a service type (e.g., “Initial Speech Evaluation”) from a dropdown, with the option prefilled when coming from a service or therapist page.

Collect child details (name, age) and show appropriate scheduling controls based on appointment type (e.g., date/time picker for specific evaluations, preferred day/time range for therapist requests).
Support selecting location (e.g., “Main Clinic - Downtown”) and available appointment times (e.g., earliest weekday after 3:00 PM between specific dates).
Capture parent contact information (name, phone, email) and submit the appointment or evaluation request.
Confirm submission with a clear success message and outline next steps (e.g., “We’ll call you within 1 business day”).

incoming_params.description: Preselected service for the appointment, such as the Speech Therapy service ID.
Internal appointment type value, e.g., “initial_speech_evaluation” or “initial_evaluation”, used to preselect the service type dropdown.
Optional preselected therapist for therapist-specific booking flows.
Optional preselected clinic location for the appointment.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a “Back to Home” or similar link after successful submission.
Click on “Find a different clinic” from within the booking form.

access_methods.description: Accessed via “Schedule”/“Request Appointment” buttons from service detail and other pages.
Preconfigured when opened with query parameters such as ?serviceId=...&appointmentType=....

pages.name: Therapist Directory
pages.description: Searchable and filterable directory of therapists to help parents find appropriate providers for their child.
primary_functions: Provide filters for specialty (e.g., Autism), age group (e.g., Teens 13–17), location, language, and insurance participation.
Allow sorting results, including by “Years of Experience (Most to Least)” to support comparing therapist experience.
Display therapist cards with name, credentials, key specialties, age groups served, years of experience, and primary location.
Allow users to click a therapist card to view detailed information on therapist_profile.html.
Optionally provide quick “Add to Favorites” controls on therapist cards.

incoming_params.description: Optional specialty filter to pre-apply, e.g., “autism”.
Optional age group value such as “teens_13_17” to prefilter therapists.
Optional sort order such as “experience_desc” to default to most experienced first.
Optional service identifier used to constrain therapist results to those providing the service.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click anywhere on a therapist card.
Click on “View Saved Therapists” link or icon in the directory header.

access_methods.description: Click on the “Our Team” item in the main header navigation.
Click the “Therapist Directory” link in the footer.
Click on “Find a Therapist” CTAs from Home or service detail pages.
Deep-linked searches using query parameters, e.g., ?specialty=autism&ageGroup=teens_13_17.

pages.name: Therapist Profile
pages.description: Detailed profile for an individual therapist, including experience, specialties, and primary clinic location.
primary_functions: Show therapist details such as bio, credentials, specialties (e.g., Autism), age ranges served (e.g., Teens 13–17), years of experience, and clinic locations.
Display a clear “Add to Favorites” or heart icon that saves the therapist to the favorites.html list.
Provide a visible “Request Appointment” button that leads to appointment_booking.html with the therapist preselected.
Offer navigation back to the therapist directory or to related services and locations.

incoming_params.description: Identifier of the therapist whose profile should be displayed.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the “Request Appointment” button on the therapist profile.
Click “Back to Therapist Directory”.
Click a link such as “See all clinics” from the profile location summary.

access_methods.description: Reached when a therapist link includes ?therapistId=... from the directory, location detail, or favorites.

pages.name: Saved Therapists
pages.description: List of therapists that the user has marked as favorites for quick access and booking.
primary_functions: Display all therapists the user has added to favorites with key info (name, specialty, years of experience).
Allow users to remove therapists from their favorites list.

Provide a “Request Appointment” or “Book with This Therapist” button for each favorite, linking to appointment_booking.html with that therapist preselected and using a simplified preferred day/time range form.
Enable users to compare saved therapists when deciding whom to choose.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a saved therapist’s name or “View Profile”.
Optional path: click on a link like “See more appointment options” after submitting the simple request.

access_methods.description: Accessed via a “Favorites” / heart icon or link in the site header tools area.
Accessed via “View Saved Therapists” links from the Therapist Directory or confirmation messages after adding a favorite.

pages.name: Parent Resources Library
pages.description: Central library of educational content (articles, videos, etc.) for parents and caregivers, including robust search and filtering options.
primary_functions: Provide a search bar allowing users to search topics such as “sensory processing.”
Offer filters for age group (e.g., “3–5 years”), content type (e.g., Articles only), and reading time (e.g., minimum 5 minutes).
Display resource cards showing title, topic tags, age range, content type, and reading time labels (e.g., “7 min read”).
Allow users to save or bookmark resources directly from the card to their reading_list.html via “Save” or bookmark icons.
Support pagination or infinite scroll with controls like “Next” to reach additional resources.

incoming_params.description: Optional search term to pre-populate the search bar, e.g., “sensory processing”.
Optional age group filter such as “ages_3_5”.
Optional content type filter, e.g., “article”.
Optional minimum reading time in minutes for pre-filtering, e.g., 5.
Page number for paginated results.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a resource card title or “Read more” link for an article.
Click “My Reading List”/“Saved Resources” link from the resources header area.

access_methods.description: Click the “Resources” item in the main header navigation.
Click the “Parent Resources” link in the footer.
Click a “Explore Resources” CTA from Home, Service Detail, or About pages.
Access with prefilled search via query parameters (topic, age group, etc.).

pages.name: Article Detail
pages.description: Full content view for an individual article in the parent resources library, with save/share tools.
primary_functions: Display the full article content including title, topic (e.g., sensory processing), age range (e.g., ages 3–5), and estimated reading time.
Provide controls to “Save to Reading List” or “Remove from Reading List” for the currently viewed article.
Allow navigation back to the filtered resources list or to related articles on similar topics.
Present print-friendly and share options if needed (e.g., print or email).

incoming_params.description: Identifier for the article resource to display.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on “Back to Resources” link.
Click on “View My Reading List” from within the article page.

access_methods.description: Accessed when an article link includes ?resourceId=... from resources or reading list pages.

pages.name: Saved Reading List
pages.description: User’s personalized list of saved or bookmarked resources and articles.
primary_functions: List all saved resources with title, topic, age range, and reading time for each.
Link each saved item back to its detail page on article_detail.html or directly open the resource.
Allow users to remove individual resources from the saved list.
Help parents quickly return to sensory processing and other educational articles they intend to read later.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a saved article title.
Click on a link like “Discover more resources”.

access_methods.description: Accessed via “My Reading List” or “Saved Resources” link/icon in the site header tools area.
Accessed from a link on the Resources page or from article confirmation messages after saving.

pages.name: Groups & Classes Listing
pages.description: Listing of group programs and classes such as social skills groups, filtered by age, date, and price.
primary_functions: Provide a search bar to look up group programs, e.g., typing “social skills.”
Offer filters for age group (e.g., “8–10 years”), date range (e.g., next full calendar month), price (e.g., maximum $50), and location if relevant.
Allow sorting by options such as “Start Date (Soonest First).”
Display group cards showing title, age range, session schedule (e.g., “Tuesdays 4:00 PM”), start date, duration, location, and price.
Enable users to click a group card to view full details on group_detail.html.

incoming_params.description: Optional search term to prefill for group search, e.g., “social skills”.
Optional age group filter such as “ages_8_10”.
Optional start date for filtering groups by date range.
Optional end date for filtering groups.
Optional maximum price filter such as 50.
Sort key such as “start_date_asc” for earliest groups first.
Optional service ID to filter groups related to a specific service.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a group/class card.

access_methods.description: Click the “Groups & Classes” item in the main header navigation.
Click the “Groups & Classes” link in the footer.
Click on group-related CTAs from Home or Service Detail pages.
Deep-linked group searches using query parameters for age, dates, price, etc.

pages.name: Group Detail
pages.description: Detail page for a specific group or class (e.g., social skills group) with schedule, pricing, and embedded registration form.
primary_functions: Present complete information on the group, including description, goals, age range (e.g., 8–10), start and end dates, session times, price, and location.
Offer a clear “Register” or “Sign Up” button that opens or scrolls to an embedded registration form on the same page.
Collect registration details including child name and age, selected session time (e.g., “Tuesdays 4:00 PM”), and parent contact information (name, phone, email).
Allow users to submit registration and display a confirmation message and follow-up expectations.

incoming_params.description: Identifier of the group program to display.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click “Back to Groups & Classes” after viewing or registering.

access_methods.description: Accessed by clicking a group card that encodes ?groupProgramId=... in the URL.

pages.name: Pricing & Insurance
pages.description: Information about service pricing, insurance plans, and an interactive multi-service cost estimation tool.
primary_functions: Outline standard rates for services like Speech Therapy and Occupational Therapy, and list accepted insurance plans (including which are in-network).
Provide a “Cost Estimator” section with an “Open Calculator” button that reveals a multi-service calculator.
Allow users to select services (e.g., Speech Therapy and Occupational Therapy) and input the number of sessions per month for each (e.g., 4 speech, 2 OT).
Include an insurance dropdown to choose plans such as “BrightCare Plus (In-Network)” and calculate an estimated monthly total.

Offer a “Request Detailed Quote” or “Talk to Us About Costs” button that reveals an inline contact form where users can describe their desired service mix and insurance, then submit a request for a detailed monthly quote.

incoming_params.description: Optional service ID used to highlight or scroll to pricing details for a specific service.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on “Schedule this service” from price tables or cost estimate results.
Click “View Services” from within the pricing overview.

access_methods.description: Click the “Pricing & Insurance” item in the main header navigation.
Click the “Pricing & Insurance” link in the footer.
Click cost-related CTAs from Home, Services, or Service Detail pages.

pages.name: Locations Finder
pages.description: Search and filter tool to help families find nearby clinics that match their needs, including amenities and weekend hours.
primary_functions: Provide a ZIP code search input (e.g., “94110”) and a “Search” button to find nearby clinics.
Offer a distance filter dropdown (e.g., “Within 15 miles”) and amenity filters such as “Free Parking.”
Include hours filters so users can select options like “Open Saturdays” or check “Saturday” availability.
Allow sorting of results by “Distance (Nearest First)” to show the closest suitable clinic.
Display clinic cards with name, address, distance, key amenities, and hours, and allow users to click a card to open location_detail.html.

incoming_params.description: Optional ZIP/postal code with which to prefill the search field.
Optional radius in miles for initial search, e.g., 15.
Whether to pre-apply the free parking filter.
Whether to pre-apply the Saturday hours filter.
Sort key such as “distance_asc” (Distance Nearest First).

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a clinic/location card.

access_methods.description: Click the “Locations” item in the main header navigation.
Click the “Clinic Locations” link in the footer.
Click “Find a Clinic Near Me” CTA from the Home page.
Access with preset filters such as ?zipCode=94110&distanceMiles=15.

pages.name: Location Detail
pages.description: Detail page for a specific clinic location, including map, hours, amenities, and callback request form.
primary_functions: Show the clinic’s name, address, map, phone number, hours (including Saturday hours if applicable), and amenities (e.g., free parking).
List key services and therapists available at this clinic with links to related pages if desired.
Provide a prominent “Request a Call” or “Have Us Call You” button leading to an inline callback request form on the page.
Allow users to enter their phone number, preferred call time (e.g., “Morning 9 AM–12 PM”), and an optional note (e.g., “Question about Saturday appointments”) and submit the request.
Offer navigation back to the locations search results or to other nearby clinics.

incoming_params.description: Identifier of the clinic location to display.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click “Back to Locations Search”.
Click on a link such as “View therapists at this clinic”.

access_methods.description: Accessed by clicking a clinic card that includes ?locationId=... in the URL.

pages.name: Weekly Planner
pages.description: Tool for creating, viewing, and saving a weekly therapy plan for a child, including multiple services across the week.
primary_functions: Allow users to start a new weekly plan via a “Create New Weekly Plan” button.

Support adding individual sessions by selecting service type (e.g., Speech Therapy, Occupational Therapy, Counseling), child name (e.g., Alex), day of week, start time, and duration (e.g., 60 minutes).
Display sessions in a structured “Week View” or “Weekly Summary” showing all planned sessions by day and time.
Enable naming and saving the plan (e.g., “Alex Spring Schedule”) via a “Save Plan” button.
Optionally allow editing or deleting sessions within the plan.

incoming_params.description: Optional ID of a previously saved weekly plan to load immediately into a draft when opening the planner.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click “Open” on one of the saved plans listed in the planner sidebar.
Click “Learn more about our services” from within the planner UI.

access_methods.description: Click the “My Planner” item in the main header navigation.
Click planner CTAs from Home or confirmation screens after scheduling.
Open with ?weeklyPlanId=... to auto-load a saved plan into the current draft.

pages.name: Donate / Support Us
pages.description: Donation and pledge page for supporting the organization’s funds such as the Therapy Scholarship Fund, with options for one-time and recurring gifts.
primary_functions: Explain giving options and impact, including specific funds like the “Therapy Scholarship Fund.”
Provide options to choose between “One-Time Gift” and recurring donations, with “One-Time Gift” selectable for pledges.
Allow users to enter a donation amount (e.g., $75) and allocate the gift to a specific fund from a dropdown.
Offer dedication options (e.g., “In honor of”) with a field for the honoree’s name (e.g., “The Garcia Family”).
Collect donor contact information (name, phone, email) and preferred contact method (e.g., Email), then submit the pledge with a confirmation message.

incoming_params.description: Optional preselected fund identifier, e.g., the Therapy Scholarship Fund.
Optional preset pledge amount when coming from a campaign CTA.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on “Learn how your gift helps” from the donation page.

access_methods.description: Click the “Support Us” item in the main header navigation.
Click the “Donate” link in the footer.
Click donation CTAs from Home or About pages.
Open with preselected fund/amount via query parameters like ?fundId=...&amount=75.

pages.name: About & Contact
pages.description: Background information about the organization, its mission, philosophy, policies, and general contact details including a simple inquiry form.
primary_functions: Present the organization’s mission, history, and therapeutic philosophy for children and families.
Provide general contact information such as main phone number, email address, mailing address, and possibly a simple general inquiry form.
Include sections (or anchors) for Privacy Policy, Terms, and Accessibility (e.g., about.html#privacy_policy, about.html#accessibility).
Offer links to core sections of the site such as Services, Our Team, Locations, and Donate.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click “View Our Therapy Services” within the About content.
Click “Meet Our Team” link.
Click “Find a Clinic” link.
Click “Support Our Work” link.

access_methods.description: Click the “About Us” link in the footer.
Click About/Contact CTAs from the Home page.

header_links.description: Homepage with quick access to all major sections
Overview of all therapy services offered
Therapist directory with filters for specialty and age group
Parent resources library with articles and guides
Group programs such as social skills groups
Find clinic locations, amenities, and hours
Service costs, insurance information, and cost estimator
Weekly therapy planning tool for your child
Donation and pledge page to support therapy funds

footer_links.description: Learn about the organization and how to contact us
Overview of therapy services and specialties
Browse and filter therapists by specialty and age group
Articles and resources for parents and caregivers
View and register for group programs and classes
Find a clinic near you with the amenities you need
Information on pricing, insurance, and cost estimates
Support our Therapy Scholarship Fund and other initiatives
